question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.timeout] Can an unprivileged external user supply an arbitrarily large value for `hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.timeout` (no `@DurationMax` constraint exists) so that `DatabaseWaiter.waitForDatabase()` enters a near-infinite `while (System.nanoTime() < deadline)` loop, holding a JVM thread for the entire duration and increasing node CPU and thread-pool resource consumption by over 30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?
